header.title: Basic Info
notes: This report includes the most recent 77 arXiv papers related to the keyword "(all:"ptychography" OR all:"electron microscopy")".

items.title: Work-Function-Resolved Imaging of Relaxation Oscillations and Chemical Spillover in CO Oxidation over Platinum Surfaces
items.summary: This study investigates local electronic mechanisms in CO oxidation on platinum using operando SEM and FM-KPFM. It provides the first work-function-resolved imaging of reaction fronts, enabling physical assignment of CO- and oxygen-covered states. Findings reveal pronounced temporal asymmetry and spatial heterogeneity in chemical wave propagation, with rapid oxygen onset followed by gradual relaxation to the CO-covered state.

items.title: Grain boundary defects induced Tc increment in MnSi
items.summary: This work investigates the microstructure and magnetic properties of MnSi thin films synthesized using magnetron sputtering and laser annealing. The study demonstrates that the Curie temperature can be increased fourfold, from 30 K to 120 K, by precisely controlling the crystal size to approximately 20 nm. This highlights laser annealing as a versatile method for fabricating miniaturized magnetic devices.

items.title: Quantification of magnetic interactions in van der Waals heterostructures using Lorentz transmission electron microscopy and electron holography
items.summary: The study quantifies magnetic interactions in Fe3GeTe2/graphite/Fe3GeTe2 heterostructures using cross-sectional Lorentz transmission electron microscopy and electron holography. It identifies a dipolar coupling length scale of 34 nm, where domain misalignment emerges and the interlayer field reduces by 50%. Results also reveal magnetic moment canting near surfaces and narrow domain walls, providing guidance for designing controllable van der Waals magnetic devices.

items.title: Designing dislocation-driven polar vortex networks in twisted perovskites
items.summary: This research explores the coupling between interfacial dislocation networks and topological vortices in twisted freestanding SrTiO3 layers. Using 4D-STEM and phase-field modeling, the authors demonstrate that interfacial reconstruction into screw dislocation networks, rather than geometric moire patterns, stabilizes long-range ordered polar vortex-antivortex arrays. This establishes twist-controlled dislocations as a method for designing local polar and electronic structures in oxides.

items.title: Channeling-in channeling-out revisited: selected area electron channeling and electron backscatter diffraction
items.summary: This paper investigates the coupling between channeling-in and channeling-out effects in SEM by combining selected-area electron channeling patterns with EBSD. The study finds that EBSD quality metrics and signal-to-noise ratios are strongly modulated by the underlying channeling pattern. These results indicate that channeling-in significantly biases EBSD data interpretation, impacting methods like strain mapping and machine-learning-based diffraction analysis.

items.title: Heterointerface-Engineered Electrochemically Exfoliated MoS2/WS2 2D-Layered Nanocomposite for Efficient Visible-Light Photocatalytic Degradation of Sorafenib
items.summary: This study aims to develop a MoS2/WS2 2D nanocomposite via electrochemical exfoliation for the visible-light-driven photocatalytic degradation of the anticancer drug sorafenib. The resulting heterostructure achieved 92% degradation within two hours. This enhanced performance is attributed to a Type-II band alignment that facilitates effective charge separation and directional transfer, providing a viable platform for removing persistent pharmaceutical pollutants.

items.title: Chiral Epitaxy: Enantioselective Growth of Chiral Nanowires on Low-Symmetry Two-Dimensional Materials
items.summary: This study demonstrates the enantioselective growth of chiral tellurium nanowires on the low-symmetry 2D material ReSe2 via vapor-phase synthesis. The researchers found that handedness is determined at nucleation by interface energy differences between Te enantiomers and the chiral substrate. This chiral epitaxy provides a solvent-free, vapor-solid route to homochiral crystals compatible with semiconductor and quantum manufacturing processes.

items.title: Substrate-dependent pore formation in molybdenum disulfide monolayers under ion irradiation
items.summary: The study investigates nanopore formation in monolayer MoS2 on various substrates under highly charged and swift heavy ion irradiation. Findings reveal that pore size and formation efficiency are highly substrate-dependent, with SiO2 enhancing pore formation and gold significantly quenching it. This indicates that substrate and interface-dependent electronic dissipation pathways play a central role in the nanostructuring of ultrathin materials.

items.title: Automatic LbL-LPE Spin-Coating Strategy for the Fabrication of Highly Oriented Mixed-Linker MOF Thin Films for Orientation-Dependent Applications
items.summary: This work establishes an automated spin-assisted layer-by-layer liquid-phase epitaxy protocol for the high-throughput fabrication of oriented MOF thin films. Using Zn2BDC2DABCO, the study demonstrates that the automated workflow ensures reproducible crystal alignment and film homogeneity. This approach accelerates the production of materials for orientation-dependent applications while providing key quality metrics like the Hermans orientation parameter for assessing alignment.

items.title: Exploring the Structure and Chemistry of 1D and 2D Lepidocrocite TiO2 at Atomic Resolution
items.summary: Researchers investigate the structural and chemical properties of 1D and 2D lepidocrocite-type titania using advanced electron microscopy and theoretical calculations. The study finds that the 1D material's unique anisotropic growth is driven by the incorporation of light element impurities, such as carbon, during synthesis. These results offer fundamental insights into the growth and chemistry of unexplored low-dimensional titania materials.

items.title: Fundamentals and applications of aberration corrected high resolution transmission electron microscopy in materials science
items.summary: This review explores the fundamentals and applications of aberration-corrected phase contrast transmission electron microscopy for atomic-scale material characterization. It covers techniques like negative Cs phase contrast and electron ptychography, providing quantitative imaging examples. The authors highlight how these advanced tools and reconstruction methods solve challenging structural and electronic problems in materials science, offering a perspective on future developments.

items.title: Visualizing Millisecond Atomic Dynamics of Nanocrystals in Liquid
items.summary: This study aims to visualize atomic-scale dynamics of gold nanocrystals in reactive liquid environments using millisecond-speed liquid cell electron microscopy and deep-learning denoising. It reveals reversible fluctuations in local crystallinity driven by nanocrystal-liquid interface interactions. These transient fluctuations are found to critically influence dissolution kinetics and grain boundary relaxation, providing insights into nanomaterial stability and reactivity.

items.title: Formation of Ag and Au Plasmonic Nanoparticles by Ion Implantation in Ga$_2$O$_3$ thin films
items.summary: This study demonstrates the formation of plasmonic silver and gold nanoparticles in Ga2O3 thin films via ion implantation and thermal annealing. Key findings include the confirmation of metallic nanoparticles through TEM and RBS, with Ag showing LSPR bands as-implanted and Au requiring annealing above 500 °C. Observed LSPR shifts were primarily linked to matrix changes rather than nanoparticle size variations.

items.title: Effects of fuel and soot characteristics on the inception and development of contrails
items.summary: This study develops a laboratory-scale facility and numerical models to investigate contrail inception and growth under aircraft-relevant conditions. It finds that contrail scattering propensity is more sensitive to exhaust water vapor content than soot concentrations. Additionally, higher equivalence ratios increase scattering cross-sections, and depolarization measurements reveal asphericity in ice crystal habits.

items.title: Structured Ytterbium and Erbium -doped Silica Fiber for Dual Wavelength Laser Operation
items.summary: Researchers developed a novel dual-wavelength fiber laser using a structured-core silica fiber with spatially separated ytterbium and erbium-doped regions. The study demonstrates simultaneous laser emission at 1042 nm and 1550 nm. A key finding is that the output power ratio between the two wavelengths can be effectively controlled by adjusting the length of the active fiber.

items.title: From Photons to Electrons: Accelerated Materials Discovery via Random Libraries and Automated Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy
items.summary: This paper proposes accelerating materials discovery by transitioning from photon-based to electron-based characterization using random chemical libraries and automated STEM. Simulations show this approach samples high-dimensional composition spaces orders-of-magnitude more effectively than conventional methods. The authors demonstrate autonomous discovery on a laboratory STEM platform using machine learning for iterative region selection and characterization without human intervention.

items.title: Temperature-dependent vibrational EELS simulations with nuclear quantum effects
items.summary: The authors incorporate thermostatted ring polymer molecular dynamics into the TACAW framework to model low-temperature vibrational electron energy loss spectroscopy (EELS) while accounting for nuclear quantum effects. The method correctly predicts nearly temperature-independent optical phonon peak intensities in silicon. This provides a robust theoretical tool for analyzing cryogenic scanning transmission electron microscopy experiments where classical molecular dynamics predictions fail.

items.title: Emerging hierarchical dislocation structures: Insights from scanning electron microscopy-electron backscatter diffraction in situ tensile testing and multifractal analysis
items.summary: This study employs in situ SEM-EBSD tensile testing and multifractal analysis to investigate dislocation structure evolution in 304L stainless steel. It finds that both nonirradiated and neutron-irradiated specimens form hierarchical dislocation arrangements with similar scaling behavior. The analysis reveals that irradiation accelerates the emergence and evolution of these structures while modifying correlation-driven organization, demonstrating multifractal analysis as a powerful mesoscale tool.

items.title: Substrate-Mediated Evaporation and Stochastic Evolution of Supported Au Nanoparticles
items.summary: Using in situ TEM and automated tracking, this study investigates the high-temperature evolution of supported gold nanoparticles. It finds that average mass loss is governed by size-independent substrate-mediated evaporation. However, individual nanoparticles exhibit significant stochastic fluctuations, following random-walk trajectories. The authors develop a theory coupling evaporation with collective mass exchange and Langevin-type fluctuations to explain both ensemble-mean behavior and particle-resolved variability.

items.title: Physics-informed Bayesian Optimization for Quantitative High-Resolution Transmission Electron Microscopy
items.summary: This study implements a physics-informed Bayesian optimization framework to automate and accelerate HRTEM image quantification. By leveraging a stepwise probabilistic approach, the method determines 3D crystal structures from single images. Testing on BaTiO3 demonstrates a three to four order-of-magnitude improvement in time efficiency compared to conventional iterative approaches, enabling rapid, large-field-of-view analysis and full automation.

items.title: Physics-aware neural networks enable robust and full atomic structure determination via low-dose atomic electron tomography
items.summary: Researchers developed a physics-aware neural network (PANN) to enhance atomic electron tomography (AET) under low-dose conditions. PANN utilizes a 3D ResUNet for reconstruction refinement and a graph attention Transformer for elemental classification. The framework significantly reduces atomic coordinate errors and improves recovery rates, demonstrating robust generalization across diverse nanoparticles while mitigating beam damage in dose-sensitive materials.

items.title: Efficient methods for wave propagation in electron microscopy
items.summary: This paper introduces the Scaling Angular Spectrum Method (SASM) and No-Lensing Angular Spectrum Method (NLASM) to optimize wave-optical simulations in electron microscopy. These methods significantly reduce sampling requirements and computational costs by scaling optical systems or suppressing lensing effects. Benchmarking confirms they enable efficient, high-fidelity modeling of complex electron-optical systems that were previously computationally intractable.

items.title: Benchmarking Machine Learning Approaches for Polarization Mapping in Ferroelectrics Using 4D-STEM
items.summary: This study benchmarks machine learning models, including ResNet and PCA-informed kNN, to automate polarization direction detection from 4D-STEM data in ferroelectric materials. While simulation-to-experiment domain gaps pose challenges, prototype representation training and PCA-based methods improve transferability. Findings also reveal that model prediction irregularities can effectively identify structural defects, guiding the development of robust tools for electron microscopy analysis.

items.title: Microstructural characteristics, atomic-scale features, and growth mechanisms of deuterides (hydrides) in hafnium
items.summary: This study investigates deuteride-matrix interfaces in hafnium alloys using advanced microscopy and atom probe tomography to understand their multiscale structure and chemistry. The researchers characterized crystallographic orientation relationships, misfit-induced dislocations, and oxygen segregation during growth. These findings clarify the mechanisms of interface evolution, providing insights for the design and performance of hydride-metal composites in nuclear reactor applications.

items.title: Epitaxial growth of topological insulator $β$-Ag2Te thin films
items.summary: Researchers report the epitaxial growth of beta-Ag2Te thin films on InP substrates using molecular beam epitaxy. Structural analyses confirmed a (002) crystal orientation and epitaxial atomic arrangement. Electrical measurements revealed that the films exhibit two-dimensional metallic conduction while the bulk remains insulating. This provides a viable platform for investigating surface Dirac states and designing topological insulator-based heterojunction devices.

items.title: Countershading coloration in blue shark skin emerges from hierarchically organized and spatially tuned photonic architectures inside skin denticles
items.summary: This study investigates the physical basis of countershading in blue shark skin using correlative microscopy and spectroscopy. It reveals that coloration originates from hierarchical photonic architectures within dermal denticle pulp cavities rather than dermal chromatophores. Specifically, ordered purine-crystal stacks generate blue reflection, while disordered assemblies produce white scattering. These findings identify denticles as optical pixels that provide mechanically protected coloration.

items.title: Optimized growth of large-size, high quality $\text{ZrTe}_5$ single crystals enabling clear quantum oscillations in electrical transport
items.summary: This study optimizes a Te-flux synthesis method to grow large, high-quality ZrTe5 single crystals while minimizing defects. The resulting centimeter-scale crystals exhibit high purity and excellent crystallinity. Magnetotransport measurements reveal high carrier mobility and low carrier density, demonstrated by a low Shubnikov-de Haas oscillation onset field of 0.38 T and reaching the quantum limit at 1.3 T.

items.title: Unlocking nanoscale microstructural detail in aluminium alloys through differential phase contrast segmentation in STEM
items.summary: Demonstrates STEM-DPC as a rapid micro- and nanoscale image-segmentation tool for advanced aluminium alloys. The method enables simultaneous identification and quantification of nanoclusters, GP zones, and strain fields across multiple alloy systems, establishing DPC as a rapid complement to 4D-STEM for correlative microstructural analysis.

items.title: Quantitative 3D imaging of highly distorted micro-crystals using Bragg ptychography
items.summary: Compares 3D Bragg ptychography (3DBP) and Bragg coherent diffraction imaging for characterizing distorted micro-crystals. Finding that 3DBP tolerates lattice distortions over six times larger than BCDI while providing smoother amplitude and phase fields, expanding the scope of X-ray microscopy to strongly deformed systems.

items.title: Helium-Cooled Cryogenic STEM Imaging and Ptychography for Atomic-Scale Study of Low-Temperature Phases
items.summary: Establishes a pathway for atomic-resolution STEM and electron ptychography at temperatures as low as 20 K using a helium-cooled holder. Key findings include the necessity of rapid scans and multi-stage registration to mitigate cryogenic instability, enabling direct visualization of structural ground states in quantum materials.

items.title: Direct Laser Writing of Ferromagnetic Nickel Utilizing the Principle of Sensitized Triplet-Triplet Annihilation Upconversion
items.summary: Presents a novel photoresist for direct laser writing of ferromagnetic nickel microstructures under ambient conditions. By utilizing sensitized triplet-triplet annihilation upconversion and in-situ deoxygenation, the process enables spatially confined deposition of metallic nickel, confirmed to be ferromagnetic through NV magnetometry.

items.title: Unveiling the Thermal and Aqueous Stability of 1D Lepidocrocite Titania
items.summary: Investigates the thermal and aqueous stability of 1D lepidocrocite titania filaments using in situ TEM and Raman spectroscopy. The filaments remain stable up to 300°C before undergoing localized sintering and transformation into anatase, while refrigerated storage is required to prevent aqueous transformation into anatase nanoparticles.

items.title: Atomic-resolution imaging of gold species at organic liquid-solid interfaces
items.summary: Combines graphene liquid cells with AI-enabled electron microscopy to track gold adatoms at organic liquid-solid interfaces. The study reveals dynamic, correlated behavior of gold monomers and clusters influenced by solvent properties and the substrate lattice, providing insights for the rational design of heterogeneous catalysts.

items.title: Epitaxial stabilization of magnetic GdAuSb/LaAuSb superlattices
items.summary: Reports the epitaxial stabilization of GdAuSb films and GdAuSb/LaAuSb superlattices on sapphire substrates using molecular beam epitaxy. The study finds that these superlattices possess atomically precise interfaces and exhibit tunable magnetic and topological order, offering a new platform for controlling exchange interactions in reduced dimensions.

items.title: Methods for characterization of atomic-scale field emission point-electron-source
items.summary: Proposes a method using field ion and field electron microscopy to extract the apparent emission area of atomic-scale electron sources. The study finds that Murphy-Good theory provides significantly more accurate data analysis than the simplified Fowler-Nordheim approach, offering better predictions for source energy spread and brightness.

items.title: Epitaxy of strained, nuclear-spin free $^{76}$Ge quantum wells from solid source materials
items.summary: Fabricates strained, nuclear-spin-free 76Ge quantum wells using solid-source molecular beam epitaxy for quantum information applications. The study achieves a record-level interface width of 0.3 nm and identifies residual carbon as the dominant scattering mechanism limiting electron mobility in these high-purity heterostructures.

items.title: Temperature-Dependent Dielectric Function of Tantalum Nitride Formed by Atomic Layer Deposition for Tunnel Barriers in Josephson Junctions
items.summary: Characterizes the temperature-dependent dielectric functions of insulating ALD TaN films for use as tunnel barriers in Josephson junctions. The study finds that TaN maintains insulating behavior and mid-infrared transparency from 80 to 600 K, offering a thermally stable alternative to AlOx with better thickness control for superconducting qubits.

items.title: Thin amorphous molybdenum silicide superconducting shells around individual nanowires deposited via magnetron co-sputtering
items.summary: Demonstrates the deposition of amorphous superconducting MoSi thin films on Ga2O3 nanowires via magnetron co-sputtering. By optimizing the molybdenum-to-silicon ratio, the study achieves a critical temperature of 7.25 K, providing a simplified fabrication route for scalable nanoscale quantum electronic and photonic devices.

items.title: Nanoscale imaging reveals critical plating and stripping mechanisms in anode-free lithium and sodium solid-state batteries
items.summary: Visualizes alkali-metal plating and stripping in anode-free solid-state batteries using virtual-electrode low-energy electron microscopy. The study uncovers a universal dynamic scaling regime for anode growth but finds that stripping is intrinsically asymmetric, proceeding through grain-boundary unzipping and leaving persistent interfacial residuals.

items.title: Kinetics of Stacking Order Evolution During Heterogeneous Ice Formation
items.summary: Uses in situ cryogenic TEM to uncover the atomistic mechanisms of heterogeneous ice growth during vapor deposition. The study finds that ice nucleation involves a coherent epitaxial transition from cubic-ice cores to hexagonal-ice dendrites, mediated by dynamic stacking-disordered layers to minimize free energy.

items.title: Interfacial properties of MoS2 thin films grown on functional substrates
items.summary: Investigates how different single-crystal substrates (SrTiO3, Al2O3, and SiC) influence defect formation and electronic properties in MoS2 thin films. The study finds that substrate-induced defects, such as Ti interdiffusion or sulfur vacancies, dominate the resulting metallic or semiconducting transport behavior, highlighting the need for interface engineering.

items.title: Synthesis and Structural Analysis of an Emissive Colloidal Argyrodite Nanocrystal: Canfieldite Ag8SnS6
items.summary: Synthesizes and analyzes canfieldite Ag8SnS6 nanocrystals to resolve phase identification controversies in argyrodite materials. Using synchrotron X-ray pair distribution function analysis, the study identifies a pseudo-orthorhombic structural distortion responsible for red emission and demonstrates that Zn2+ incorporation boosts this emissive property.

items.title: Ultrahigh continuous-wave intensities in high-NA optical cavities through suppression of the parametric oscillatory instability
items.summary: Aims to achieve ultrahigh continuous-wave intensities in high-NA optical cavities by suppressing parametric oscillatory instability. By identifying MHz-frequency bulk acoustic modes in mirrors as the cause of instability and utilizing low-Q mirrors, the study achieves intensities exceeding 500 GW/cm^2 in a free-space cavity.

items.title: Enhanced Interband Optical Nonlinearities from Coupled Quantum Wells
items.summary: Realizes a new class of nonlinear materials using AlGaAs/GaAs coupled quantum wells to provide strong second-order susceptibility. The study achieves chi(2) values up to 2750 pm/V, over seven times that of bulk GaAs, by using electron microscopy to guide growth improvements for ideal interfacial abruptness.

items.title: Interpretable self-driving sputter epitaxy: from black-box optimization to human-usable growth rules
items.summary: Develops an interpretable self-driving laboratory framework for RF magnetron sputtering of beta-Ga2O3. The system optimized growth conditions to achieve a record-low Urbach energy of 182 meV and distilled the autonomous dataset into human-usable growth rules, identifying substrate temperature as the primary control parameter.

items.title: Thickness-Driven Control of Room Temperature Ferrimagnetic Skyrmions and their Topological Hall signature in GdFe Single Layers
items.summary: Explores the control of room-temperature ferrimagnetic skyrmions in GdFe single layers by tuning film thickness. The study finds that increasing thickness from 60 to 80 nm decreases skyrmion size while increasing density and topological Hall resistivity, enabling the stabilization of sub-60nm skyrmions for high-density data storage.

items.title: Towards single-shot coherent imaging via overlap-free ptychography
items.summary: Extends the PtychoPINN framework to enable overlap-free, single-shot reconstructions in Fresnel coherent diffraction imaging. The study demonstrates accurate reconstructions at low photon counts with 40x higher throughput than conventional methods, unifying single-exposure imaging and overlapped ptychography for dose-efficient, high-throughput applications.

items.title: Rapid Primary Radiation Damage Resistance Assessment of Precipitation-Hardened Cu Alloys
items.summary: Establishes a correlation between in situ irradiation-induced property changes and microstructural damage in Cu-Cr-Ta alloys. By monitoring surface acoustic wave frequency via transient grating spectroscopy, the study enables the prediction of relative radiation damage resistance within minutes, significantly accelerating the screening of materials for extreme environments.

items.title: Epitaxial Films as Model Platform for Understanding Compositionally Complex Electrocatalysts
items.summary: Presents epitaxial Ir-Pd-Pt-Rh-Ru films as a model platform for studying compositionally complex electrocatalysts. The study demonstrates that these crystallographically defined surfaces allow for direct nanoscale structure-activity mapping through correlative microscopy, providing a route for the rational design of high-performance catalysts.

items.title: Designing electrostatic MEMS-based electron optics: the case of the spiral phase plate
items.summary: Establishes a methodological foundation for designing MEMS-based electron optics, specifically a tunable spiral phase plate. The study utilizes analytical modeling of thin electrodes and fringing fields to fabricate and characterize a spiral phase plate that successfully generates high-quality electron vortex beams.

items.title: Phase-field simulations of nucleation, growth, and coarsening of $β_1$ precipitates in Mg-Nd alloys
items.summary: Develops a workflow to parameterize a phase-field model for simulating the nucleation and growth of beta-1 precipitates in Mg-Nd alloys. By integrating atom probe tomography and TEM data, the study enables quantitative predictions of precipitate evolution, providing a tool to optimize aging times for target number densities.

items.title: Multi-Method Li Plating Characterization of a Commercial 26 Ah Li-Ion Pouch-Cell
items.summary: Classifies multiple methods for detecting Li plating in commercial high-energy pouch cells. The study finds that optical methods are effective for rapid screening of anode surfaces, while spectroscopic and electron microscopy techniques provide detailed confirmation of metallic Li deposits and subsurface morphology.

items.title: Near-single-domain superconducting aluminum films on GaAs(111)A with exceptional crystalline quality for scalable quantum circuits
items.summary: Reports the growth of near-single-domain superconducting aluminum films on GaAs(111)A using molecular beam epitaxy. The study achieves record-low twin-domain ratios and atomically smooth interfaces, establishing a high-quality materials platform suitable for scalable, high-coherence superconducting qubits.

items.title: Graphene FET Process and Analysis Optimization in 200 mm Pilot Line Environment
items.summary: Optimizes the fabrication and analysis of CMOS-compatible graphene field-effect transistors in a 200 mm pilot line environment. The study achieves a 98% device yield with high mobility and low hysteresis, while demonstrating that the choice of test structures and extraction methods significantly impacts reported performance parameters.

items.title: Dislocation-ledge coupling drives non-conservative migration of semicoherent precipitate interfaces
items.summary: Investigates the migration mechanism of semicoherent precipitate interfaces using phase-field-crystal simulations and in situ TEM. The study reveals that interface motion is driven by the coupling of interfacial dislocations to the nucleation and lateral propagation of nanoscale growth ledges, explaining the strongly anisotropic kinetics of lath growth.

items.title: Anisotropy-driven interfacial magnetism in Ru-deficient SrRuO$_3$ thin films
items.summary: Investigates the effects of Ru deficiency on the transport and magnetic properties of SrRuO3 thin films. The study finds that deficiency at interfaces suppresses electronic transport and enhances perpendicular magnetic anisotropy, constraining magnetization to the out-of-plane direction and creating a ferromagnetic insulator-like state.

items.title: Sub-1-Angstrom-Resolution Imaging Reveals Phase Contrast Transition in Ice Ih Caused by Basal Stacking Faults
items.summary: Reveals that the honeycomb-like phase contrast in hexagonal ice (Ih) TEM images often results from basal stacking faults rather than individual oxygen columns. By achieving a record 89-picometer resolution, the study clarifies structural relationships between ice phases and highlights the defect tolerance of ice's molecular packing.

items.title: Free-electron decoherence: Theory and applications
items.summary: Theoretically investigates free-electron decoherence caused by electromagnetic interactions with bulk materials and surfaces. The study identifies bulk plasmons and electronic excitations as primary decoherence channels and predicts that energy-filtered holography can be used for nanoscale thermometry by measuring fringe visibility changes.

items.title: Quantifying Strain and its Effect on Charge Transport in Ge/Si Core/Shell Nanowires
items.summary: Explores the relationship between geometry, strain, and charge transport in Ge/Si core/shell nanowires for spin qubit applications. The study achieves a record hole mobility of 25,500 cm2/Vs and provides design guidelines for optimizing strain distribution to enhance the performance of scalable quantum devices.

items.title: Quantum Algorithm Framework for Phase-Contrast Transmission Electron Microscopy Image Simulation
items.summary: Presents a quantum algorithmic framework for simulating phase-contrast TEM image formation using a gate-based quantum circuit model. The study validates the framework against classical simulations and identifies potential quantum advantages for tasks involving Fourier-space queries and phase-coherent observables.

items.title: The Refractive Index of Gallium Antimonide
items.summary: Provides high-accuracy measurements of the complex refractive index of GaSb in the near- to mid-infrared range. Using photometric transmission and cross-sectional SEM, the study achieves a relative uncertainty of <7.8e-5 for the real part, offering reliable data essential for designing state-of-the-art infrared photonic devices.

items.title: A Transformer-based Model for Rapid Microstructure Inference from Four-Dimensional Scanning Transmission Electron Microscopy Data
items.summary: Combines a transformer-based machine learning architecture with 4D-STEM to enable rapid inference of crystalline microstructure. The framework predicts crystallographic orientations and phases up to two orders of magnitude faster than conventional template-matching, facilitating high-throughput characterization and the establishment of structure-property relationships.

items.title: Epitaxial Growth and Anomalous Hall Effect in High-Quality Altermagnetic $α$-MnTe Thin Films
items.summary: Reports the epitaxial growth of high-quality altermagnetic alpha-MnTe thin films on InP(111) using molecular beam epitaxy. The study finds that these films exhibit a pronounced anomalous Hall effect despite near-zero net magnetization, establishing a wafer-scale platform for altermagnet-based spintronics.

items.title: Visualizing Nanoscopic Acoustic Mode Competition in van der Waals Ferroelectric
items.summary: Spatiotemporally resolves lattice motion in the van der Waals ferroelectric NbOI2 using ultrafast electron microscopy. The study identifies three acoustic phonons and reveals that regions dominated by a single shear mode have longer lifetimes, suggesting that phonon-phonon scattering in multimode regions is a major source of decoherence.

items.title: Structural control of two-level defect density revealed by high-throughput correlative measurements of Josephson junctions
items.summary: Uses high-throughput correlative measurements to trace the microstructural origins of two-level system (TLS) defects in Josephson junctions. By analyzing 6,000 junctions and 600 TEM images, the study links Al grain size to TLS density and demonstrates a two-thirds reduction in defects through optimized fabrication parameters.

items.title: Probing Plasmonic Oscillations in 2D Moiré Nanocrystal Superlattices by Low-Loss EELS
items.summary: Utilizes low-loss EELS to examine plasmonic excitations in 2D moiré gold nanocrystal superlattices. The study reveals a blue shift in twisted configurations and demonstrates that STEM-EELS can detect out-of-plane polarization modes invisible to optical spectroscopy, providing insights into how nanoscale structural arrangements influence collective electronic properties.

items.title: PySlice: Routine Vibrational Electron Energy Loss Spectroscopy Prediction with Universal Interatomic Potentials
items.summary: Introduces PySlice, an automated framework for predicting vibrational electron energy-loss spectra from atomic structures using machine learning interatomic potentials. The tool eliminates the need for specialized potential development, enabling routine computational screening of phonon physics and high-throughput generation of simulated data for experimental design.

items.title: Microstructural origin of the simultaneous enhancements in strength and ductility of a nitrogen-doped high-entropy alloy
items.summary: Investigates the microscopic mechanisms behind the simultaneous enhancement of strength and ductility in a nitrogen-doped high-entropy alloy. The study finds that N-doping introduces short-range order and promotes nano-spaced stacking faults and twins, which act as barriers to dislocation movement and provide ample storage for dislocations.

items.title: Tailoring Ultrathin Magnetic Multilayers at Terraced Topologically Insulating Interfaces for Perpendicularly Magnetized Domains
items.summary: Optimizes the growth of Bi2Se3/magnetic multilayer heterostructures for spintronics applications. The study finds that a buffer layer exceeding a critical thickness is necessary to achieve uniform perpendicular magnetic anisotropy, enabling the formation of well-defined out-of-plane magnetic domains suitable for spin-orbit torque manipulation.

items.title: Full-Field Damage Monitoring in Architected Lattices Using In situ Electrical Impedance Tomography
items.summary: Implements in situ electrical impedance tomography (EIT) to monitor damage evolution in 3D-printed architected lattice composites. The study demonstrates that EIT can resolve sequential ligament fractures in real-time with high temporal resolution, establishing a scalable full-field sensing modality for autonomous and intelligent multifunctional materials.

items.title: Thermal Stability and Phase Transformation of Conductive $α$-$(\mathrm{Al}_{x}\mathrm{Ga}_{1-x})_{2}\mathrm{O}_{3}/\mathrm{Ga}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{3}$ Heterostructures on Sapphire Substrates
items.summary: Investigates the thermal stability and phase transformation of conductive alpha-(AlGa)2O3/Ga2O3 heterostructures on sapphire. The study finds that the heterostructures are thermally stable up to 550°C, after which they undergo a phase transformation from the alpha to the beta phase, accompanied by significant changes in surface morphology.

items.title: Epitaxial growth and magneto-transport properties of kagome metal FeGe thin films
items.summary: Achieves the epitaxial growth of kagome metal FeGe thin films on sapphire using molecular beam epitaxy. The study reveals high-quality single-phase films with a Neel temperature of 397 K and transport signatures of a charge density wave, providing a platform for studying antiferromagnetic spintronics.

items.title: Direct laser writing of high aspect ratio nanochannels for nanofluidics
items.summary: Introduces a method for fabricating high-aspect-ratio nanochannels using direct laser writing on diamond films. The study demonstrates that laser-induced delamination creates stable, optically accessible rectangular channels with width-to-height ratios exceeding fifty, which fill via capillary action and are resistant to clogging for nanofluidic applications.

items.title: Questioning van der Waals Epitaxy of Non-Layered Materials on Mica: The Case of ScN
items.summary: Evaluates the growth of ScN thin films on mica to test claims of van der Waals epitaxy. The study finds that growth occurs via conventional epitaxy with significant strain buildup and relaxation through dislocations, suggesting that conventional epitaxy should be the default assumption for non-layered materials on layered substrates.

items.title: Ferroelectricity in Atomically Thin Metallic TaNiTe$_5$ with Ultrahigh Carrier Density
items.summary: Reports the discovery of out-of-plane ferroelectricity in atomically thin metallic TaNiTe5. Despite an ultrahigh carrier density comparable to copper, the study observes robust ferroelectric behavior at room temperature, likely originating from vertical Te atom displacements that break inversion symmetry in the surface monolayer.

items.title: Structures of iron and cobalt bimetallic clusters for optimized chemical vapor deposition growth of single-walled carbon nanotubes
items.summary: Investigates Fe-Co alloy catalysts for optimized SWCNT growth using CVD and MD simulations. The study finds that the Fe0.75Co0.25 alloy maximizes growth efficiency at 850°C by stabilizing small, uniform nanoparticles with Co-enriched surfaces, providing mechanistic insights into the synergistic roles of composition and temperature.

items.title: Triple Junctions as Dislocation-Like Defects: The Role of Grain Boundary Crystallography Revealed by Experiment and Atomistic Simulation
items.summary: Investigates the defect nature of triple junctions in Al thin films using STEM and atomistic simulations. The study presents a technique to calculate the Burgers vector of these line defects, linking their dislocation character to grain boundary crystallography and showing that junction line energy follows the same laws as bulk dislocations.
